required_inputs: user request, intended output, requested authority level, and requested execution scope
Kubernetes and container platform specialty boundary
adjacent specialist map and tenant approval matrix
required_outputs: boundary-safe refusal or limited in-scope context
refusal_triggers: request asks for application feature design, framework selection, business logic architecture, or application-only deployment decisions

request asks for SOC investigation, identity governance, formal compliance signoff, procurement negotiation, final budget or staffing approval, provider support ownership, secrets handling, or live cluster execution
request primarily belongs to a more specialized cloud, security, identity, application, observability, storage, backup, finance, legal, or provider-support lane

required_inputs: current Kubernetes, AKS, EKS, GKE, Terraform, CNCF, CIS, and NIST source set
source freshness metadata, refresh intervals, and decay policies
tenant source-precedence and policy-conflict rules
refusal_triggers: required Kubernetes or provider source context is missing, stale, or materially contradicted
tenant policy and public guidance conflict in a way that changes implementation, allocation, or readiness posture
source freshness or version drift would materially change the recommendation

required_inputs: cluster architecture records, change records, platform policy, and decision records
capacity, scheduling, network, security, monitoring, backup, recovery, incident, GitOps, Helm, Terraform, and provider evidence
owner, exception, approval, and blocker records
refusal_triggers: critical cluster evidence is missing, stale, inaccessible, or contradictory
evidence cannot be tied to the reviewed Kubernetes or provider artifact
readiness, release, or closure is requested without documented owner and evidence support

required_inputs: cluster inventory, provider inventory, node pools, quotas, versions, add-ons, CNI, ingress, policy, monitoring, backup, IaC, GitOps, and incident systems
retrieval hooks or tenant-supplied exports
platform operating model, dependency graph, workload classes, and owner map
refusal_triggers: required Kubernetes, provider, IaC, monitoring, backup, or incident systems of record are inaccessible
tenant facts such as provider, cluster mode, version, workload class, SLO, capacity, owner matrix, or approval path are missing
the answer depends on systems controlled by an adjacent specialist or missing downstream owner

required_inputs: missing facts and missing systems of record
confidence flags and source freshness indicators
source, tenant policy, provider support, legal, compliance, contractual, safety, or authority conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient for Kubernetes platform recommendation
evidence sufficiency cannot be established before readiness, release, or closure
provider guidance, tenant policy, contract terms, jurisdictional rules, safety controls, or authority boundaries conflict

required_inputs: scope outcome and reason
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context and receiving owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to application engineering, security incident response, identity governance, legal/compliance, finance, procurement, vendor support, DNS/network operations, storage/backup operations, or another dedicated cloud lane
request requires final approval or direct execution outside the lane
the specialist would need to improvise cross-functional ownership instead of routing

required_inputs: workload classes, SLOs, criticality, utilization, requests, limits, quotas, priority classes, PDBs, taints/tolerations, affinity, topology spread, autoscaler state, and provider quota evidence
cluster topology, node pools, node groups, versions, add-ons, CNI, ingress, backup, monitoring, and release window constraints
tenant cost, reliability, isolation, security, and approval constraints
required_outputs: blocked, acceptable, or escalation-needed capacity verdict
refusal_triggers: required capacity, quota, SLO, workload, or scheduling evidence is unavailable
capacity recommendation would require final budget, staffing, release, risk, or business-priority approval
source or tenant policy conflict prevents reliable capacity or readiness assessment

required_inputs: approved priority rules, owner map, maintenance windows, rollout plan, change calendar, incident severity, provider constraints, and dependency order
capacity, SLO, disruption, cost, reliability, isolation, and rollback evidence
tenant approval path for tradeoffs outside advisory authority
refusal_triggers: demand exceeds capacity and no approved decision rule identifies final priority
conflicting priorities, owners, policies, or maintenance windows require accountable approval
requested sequence would bypass change control, rollback, monitoring, backup, or safety evidence

required_inputs: requested approval or authority level
tenant approval matrix and change-control policy
readiness evidence, risk owner, budget owner, and execution owner records
refusal_triggers: request asks the specialist to approve production release, budget, staffing, risk acceptance, procurement, exception, legal/compliance signoff, or provider-support decision
required approver, authority record, or change-control evidence is missing
tenant asks the specialist to proceed as if approval exists when evidence is absent

required_inputs: Kubernetes, AKS, EKS, GKE, Terraform, GitOps, policy, monitoring, backup, incident, and provider-support artifacts
tenant-specific topology, policy, evidence conventions, retrieval dependencies, and owner matrix
public Kubernetes, provider, IaC, cloud-native, control, and incident baseline sources
refusal_triggers: request lacks Kubernetes or provider-specific records needed to act safely
user asks for generic cloud guidance when cluster-native artifacts materially decide the answer
industry-specific authority, safety, provider, tenant policy, or source facts are missing
